message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should apply 20% loyalty discount when customer has >= 2 years with MHPCO"
Expected: 2-year customer + plain sword → 100 + 10 - 20 + 5 = 95
Current error: expected 95, received 115
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Apply 20% loyalty discount to the POLICY BASE PREMIUM (sum of all item base premiums) when customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= 2. So the discount = 0.2 * sum(item base premiums). Subtract from total.

Per spec: item-specific surcharges apply to item bases; policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first-insurance applied here is per-item, follow-up) apply to policy base premium. First-insurance is per-item.

So loyalty discount = 0.2 * (sum of item base premiums), subtracted from policy total. Verify: 100 base + 10 first-insurance - 20 loyalty(=0.2*100) + 5 fee = 95. ✓

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal code to make the failing test pass.
I'll acknowledge the available skills but they're not relevant to this TDD Green phase task. Now let me implement the minimal change to apply the loyalty discount.

content.text:
All 17 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added loyalty discount logic. When `customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= 2`, compute 20% of the policy base premium (sum of all item base premiums, using component block-pricing for components) and subtract it from the total before rounding. Threaded `yearsWithMHPCO` from the scenario into `quotePremium`.
- Result: All tests now pass (17 passing, 22 todo).
- Approach: The discount applies to the sum of item base premiums (per spec), not to the full per-item premium with surcharges. Extracted `policyBasePremium` to compute that sum cleanly. For the loyalty test case: 100 base + 10 first-insurance - 20 loyalty(=0.2*100) + 5 fee = 95.
